text: Last summer, I planned a trip to Japan with two friends. We spent weeks researching destinations and booking flights. Once there, everything felt new—the bustling streets of Tokyo, the quiet temples of Kyoto. The food was amazing; we tried ramen, sushi, and even octopus balls. However, language barriers were tricky. Sometimes, we used hand gestures or a translation app to order meals. Despite the challenges, these moments became the best memories. Experiencing a different culture taught me to appreciate both the similarities and differences in how people live. I still smile when I think about the kindness of strangers and the delicious flavors we discovered.
